originContent: # claude-sbox
translatedContent: # claude-sbox

originContent: A [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com) skill that teaches Claude to write idiomatic [s&box](https://sbox.game) code — C# components, Razor UI, physics, and networking — without falling back on Unity patterns.
translatedContent: A [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com) skill that teaches Claude to write idiomatic [s&box](https://sbox.game) code — C# components, Razor UI, physics, and networking — without falling back on Unity patterns.

originContent: s&box is Facepunch's Source 2 game engine with a C# scripting layer. Its API, lifecycle, and networking model are *nothing like* Unity's, which means stock Claude hallucinates constantly: `void Start()`, `GetComponent<T>()` in the Unity sense, `Physics.Raycast`, `[SerializeField]`, `StartCoroutine` — all wrong in s&box. This skill loads when you're writing s&box code and redirects Claude to the correct APIs, verified against the engine's exported schema.
translatedContent: s&box is Facepunch's Source 2 game engine with a C# scripting layer. Its API, lifecycle, and networking model are *nothing like* Unity's, which means stock Claude hallucinates constantly: `void Start()`, `GetComponent<T>()` in the Unity sense, `Physics.Raycast`, `[SerializeField]`, `StartCoroutine` — all wrong in s&box. This skill loads when you're writing s&box code and redirects Claude to the correct APIs, verified against the engine's exported schema.

originContent: ## Install
translatedContent: ## Install

originContent: **Personal (available across all your projects):**
translatedContent: **Personal (available across all your projects):**

originContent: ```bash
translatedContent: ```bash

originContent: mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills
translatedContent: mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills

originContent: git clone https://github.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox ~/.claude/skills/sbox
translatedContent: git clone https://github.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox ~/.claude/skills/sbox

originContent: **Project-local (this game only):**
translatedContent: **Project-local (this game only):**

originContent: ```bash
translatedContent: ```bash

originContent: cd my-sbox-game
translatedContent: cd my-sbox-game

originContent: mkdir -p .claude/skills
translatedContent: mkdir -p .claude/skills

originContent: git clone https://github.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox .claude/skills/sbox
translatedContent: git clone https://github.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox .claude/skills/sbox

Claude Code picks up skill file changes live. However, if `~/.claude/skills/` did not exist when your Claude Code session started (i.e. this is your first personal skill), you need to **restart Claude Code** after the `mkdir` so the watcher registers the new directory.

originContent: Claude Code picks up skill file changes live. However, if `~/.claude/skills/` did not exist when your Claude Code session started (i.e. this is your first personal skill), you need to **restart Claude Code** after the `mkdir` so the watcher registers the new directory.

translatedContent: > **Why the `sbox` (not `claude-sbox`) directory name?** The `name:` frontmatter in `SKILL.md` is `sbox`, which becomes the `/sbox` slash command. Cloning into `~/.claude/skills/sbox/` keeps the directory name and the invocation name in sync.

originContent: > **Why the `sbox` (not `claude-sbox`) directory name?** The `name:` frontmatter in `SKILL.md` is `sbox`, which becomes the `/sbox` slash command. Cloning into `~/.claude/skills/sbox/` keeps the directory name and the invocation name in sync.

translatedContent: ## Verify it's working

originContent: ## Verify it's working

translatedContent: In any Claude Code session, type:

originContent: In any Claude Code session, type:
/sbox
translatedContent: /sbox
The router loads. Alternatively, ask a triggering question and watch Claude reach for a reference file:

originContent: The router loads. Alternatively, ask a triggering question and watch Claude reach for a reference file:
How do I write a networked player controller in s&box?
translatedContent: How do I write a networked player controller in s&box?

originContent: Claude should open `references/core-concepts.md`, `references/networking.md`, and/or `references/patterns-and-examples.md` before answering — *that's the signal the skill is working*. If it answers from memory without reading a file, something's wrong; see [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting).
translatedContent: Claude should open `references/core-concepts.md`, `references/networking.md`, and/or `references/patterns-and-examples.md` before answering — *that's the signal the skill is working*. If it answers from memory without reading a file, something's wrong; see [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting).

originContent: ## What's inside
translatedContent: ## What's inside

originContent: `SKILL.md` is a router, not a reference. When Claude needs detail, it opens one of these:
translatedContent: `SKILL.md` is a router, not a reference. When Claude needs detail, it opens one of these:

originContent: | File | Lines | Covers |
translatedContent: | File | Lines | Covers |

originContent: | `SKILL.md` | 271 | Router + Unity→s&box translation table + the ten rules |
translatedContent: | `SKILL.md` | 271 | Router + Unity→s&box translation table + the ten rules |

originContent: | `references/core-concepts.md` | 575 | Scene system, GameObjects, Components, lifecycle, `[Property]`, prefabs, scene events, `GameObjectSystem`, async |
translatedContent: | `references/core-concepts.md` | 575 | Scene system, GameObjects, Components, lifecycle, `[Property]`, prefabs, scene events, `GameObjectSystem`, async |

originContent: | `references/components-builtin.md` | 729 | 144 built-in components — renderers, rigidbodies, colliders, `CharacterController`, `CameraComponent`, lights, audio, UI, `NavMeshAgent`, `PlayerController`, particles, post-processing |
translatedContent: | `references/components-builtin.md` | 729 | 144 built-in components — renderers, rigidbodies, colliders, `CharacterController`, `CameraComponent`, lights, audio, UI, `NavMeshAgent`, `PlayerController`, particles, post-processing |

originContent: Every API signature in every reference file is verified against the s&box engine's exported schema (`raw/api-schema.json`, ~1,850 types across 61 namespaces). Schema is the single source of truth — if the docs and the schema disagree, the schema wins.
translatedContent: Every API signature in every reference file is verified against the s&box engine's exported schema (`raw/api-schema.json`, ~1,850 types across 61 namespaces). Schema is the single source of truth — if the docs and the schema disagree, the schema wins.

originContent: ## Regenerating from source
translatedContent: ## Regenerating from source

originContent: End users don't need this. For maintainers who want to rebuild the reference files against a newer s&box engine:
translatedContent: End users do not need this. For maintainers who want to rebuild the reference files against a newer s&box engine:

originContent: ```bash
translatedContent: ```bash

originContent: ./scripts/fetch-raw.sh         # clones Facepunch/sbox-docs into raw/sbox-docs
translatedContent: ./scripts/fetch-raw.sh         # clones Facepunch/sbox-docs into raw/sbox-docs

originContent: # manually place raw/api-schema.json (see docs/DESIGN.md)
translatedContent: # manually place raw/api-schema.json (see docs/DESIGN.md)

originContent: node scripts/build_extended.js # rebuilds references/api-schema-extended.md
translatedContent: node scripts/build_extended.js # rebuilds references/api-schema-extended.md

The full build workflow — including how each reference file was curated, the schema-verification loop, and known gotchas — is documented in [`docs/DESIGN.md`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox/main/docs/DESIGN.md) and [`docs/BUILD_LOG.md`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox/main/docs/BUILD_LOG.md).

originContent: The full build workflow — including how each reference file was curated, the schema-verification loop, and known gotchas — is documented in [`docs/DESIGN.md`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox/main/docs/DESIGN.md) and [`docs/BUILD_LOG.md`](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gavogavogavo/claude-sbox/main/docs/BUILD_LOG.md).

translatedContent: ## Troubleshooting

originContent: ## Troubleshooting

translatedContent: **Claude isn't triggering the skill on s&box questions.**

originContent: **Claude isn't triggering the skill on s&box questions.**
translatedContent: Check that the directory is at `~/.claude/skills/sbox/` (not `~/.claude/skills/claude-sbox/` or `~/.claude/skills/sbox-skill/`). The directory name must match the `name:` frontmatter. Also try invoking it explicitly with `/sbox` to confirm it's installed.

originContent: Check that the directory is at `~/.claude/skills/sbox/` (not `~/.claude/skills/claude-sbox/` or `~/.claude/skills/sbox-skill/`). The directory name must match the `name:` frontmatter. Also try invoking it explicitly with `/sbox` to confirm it's installed.

translatedContent: **Claude answers s&box questions without opening a reference file.**

originContent: **Claude answers s&box questions without opening a reference file.**
translatedContent: That means it's hallucinating from Unity muscle memory — the exact failure this skill exists to prevent. Either the skill isn't loading, or the description isn't matching. Try `/sbox` to force-load it, then retry the question.

originContent: That means it's hallucinating from Unity muscle memory — the exact failure this skill exists to prevent. Either the skill isn't loading, or the description isn't matching. Try `/sbox` to force-load it, then retry the question.

translatedContent: **The skill is loading but suggesting APIs that don't compile.**

originContent: **The skill is loading but suggesting APIs that don't compile.**
translatedContent: Open an issue with the suggested code and which reference file Claude claims it came from. Every shipped signature should be schema-verified; regressions are bugs.

originContent: Open an issue with the suggested code and which reference file Claude claims it came from. Every shipped signature should be schema-verified; regressions are bugs.

translatedContent: **I cloned it into `~/.claude/skills/claude-sbox/` — now what?**

originContent: **I cloned it into `~/.claude/skills/claude-sbox/` — now what?**
translatedContent: Either rename the directory to `sbox`, or change the `name:` field in `SKILL.md` to `claude-sbox` to match. Same deal for project-local installs.

originContent: Either rename the directory to `sbox`, or change the `name:` field in `SKILL.md` to `claude-sbox` to match. Same deal for project-local installs.

translatedContent: ## Contributing

originContent: ## Contributing

translatedContent: Issues and PRs welcome. Before submitting:

originContent: Issues and PRs welcome. Before submitting:

translatedContent: - **Verify new API signatures against `raw/api-schema.json`.** The schema is ground truth. If you can't find a method in the schema, don't add it.

originContent: - **Verify new API signatures against `raw/api-schema.json`.** The schema is ground truth. If you can't find a method in the schema, don't add it.
translatedContent: - **Keep `SKILL.md` under 500 lines.** It's a router — reference detail lives in `references/`. If you can answer an s&box question using only `SKILL.md` without opening a reference file, the router has too much detail.

originContent: - **Keep `SKILL.md` under 500 lines.** It's a router — reference detail lives in `references/`. If you can answer an s&box question using only `SKILL.md` without opening a reference file, the router has too much detail.
translatedContent: - **Update `CHANGELOG.md`** for any user-visible change.

originContent: - **Update `CHANGELOG.md`** for any user-visible change.
translatedContent: - **Match the existing prose style** — terse, dense, written for Claude rather than for a tutorial reader.

originContent: - **Match the existing prose style** — terse, dense, written for Claude rather than for a tutorial reader.
